agent.name: performance-cost-auditor
agent.role: Find performance bottlenecks, unnecessary cost drivers, and efficiency gaps across database queries, frontend loading, caching, and API usage.

findings.title: Unoptimized SELECT * Queries
findings.description: Multiple Supabase queries use .select('*') to fetch all columns. This wastes bandwidth and memory, especially for tables with large text or JSONB fields like projects and task_prompts.

proof_hooks.summary: Unspecified column selection with .select('*') in adapter files
Expected explicit column list, found wildcard selector

findings.impact: Unnecessary bandwidth and memory consumption on every project query; increases Supabase compute cost at scale and slows response times for large JSONB payloads.

suggested_fix.approach: Explicitly specify only the required columns in the .select() chain for all Supabase queries. Audit adapter.ts files in src/lib/api to replace .select('*') with column whitelists matching the consuming component's data needs.

history.notes: Detected during static analysis of Supabase adapter files.

findings.title: Missing staleTime in React Query Hooks
findings.description: Almost all project hooks (useProjects, useProjectTasks, etc.) omit the staleTime configuration. With the default of 0ms, this triggers redundant network requests on every window focus, component remount, and network reconnect.

proof_hooks.summary: useQuery calls in project hooks lack staleTime configuration
Expected staleTime in useQuery config, found no staleTime property
proof_hooks.actual: Missing staleTime property in useQuery config object

findings.impact: Redundant API and database calls on every window focus and remount; increases Supabase read usage and degrades perceived UI responsiveness for users switching between tabs or navigating back.

suggested_fix.approach: Configure an appropriate staleTime (e.g., 1-5 minutes) for data that does not change frequently. Apply to all useQuery hooks in src/lib/api/projects/hooks.ts and any other hooks that fetch relatively stable server data.

history.notes: Detected during review of React Query hook configurations.

findings.title: Missing Code Splitting for Large Libraries
findings.description: The application relies on heavy dependencies (monaco-editor, @xyflow/react, html2canvas, jspdf, recharts) but lacks any React.lazy() or dynamic import() code-splitting boundaries. This forces all users to download the full weight of these libraries upfront, severely impacting initial load performance.

proof_hooks.summary: src/App.tsx imports heavy libraries statically with no lazy boundaries
vite.config.ts lacks manualChunks configuration for heavy dependencies
No dynamic imports found; all dependencies statically compiled to main bundle
proof_hooks.expected: Dynamic chunks for editor and flow diagram components
proof_hooks.actual: No dynamic imports () found in src code; large dependencies are statically compiled to the main bundle.

findings.impact: All users pay the full bundle download cost on first load regardless of which features they use; significantly increases Time-to-Interactive and Largest Contentful Paint scores.

suggested_fix.approach: Wrap heavy, route-specific components (editor, flow diagram, PDF export) in React.lazy() and Suspense boundaries to lazily load expensive libraries. Configure Vite manualChunks in vite.config.ts to split monaco-editor, @xyflow/react, and recharts into separate async chunks.

history.notes: Detected during static import analysis across App.tsx and vite.config.ts.

next_actions: Audit SELECT * queries in adapter files and replace with specific column whitelists.
Add staleTime configuration to all React Query useQuery hooks.
Implement React.lazy() for route-level code splitting, especially for the IDE/Editor and Flow routes.
